Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 399
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Continued To Sep-27-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANTS MARLON YOUNG AND MERRILL, ARNONE & JONES'S MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO CCP 399

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Marlon Young and Merrill, Arnone & Jones's motion to dismiss pursuant to CCP 399 is continued to September 27, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. If plaintiff Carl Wescott does not pay the transfer fees or obtain a waiver for the transfer fees by the time of the September 27 hearing, the case will be dismissed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)

Mtn To Compel In Support Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Req.'S For Production
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production.

Off-calendar. The case was ordered transferred to Sonoma County. Plaintiff to immediately pay all transfer fees or seek fee relief/waiver from the court.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Diana R. Kaempfer of the LaFollette Johnson, Tel. No. 1-916-563-3100 appearing
for the Defendants;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Marlon Young and Merrill, Arnone & Jones' motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to Sonoma County because that is where the defendants reside. (CCP 395). Plaintiff Carl Wescott is required to pay all required transfer fees. The fact that the Arnovick agreement required payment in San Francisco or that the defendants represented full payment to the bankruptcy trustee in San Francisco is irrelevant to the venue issue. Mr. Wescott and the defendants have no contractual relationship and Mr. Wescott has not sued the moving defendants for breach of contract. Mr. Wescott's tort claims are properly venued at defendants' residences in Sonoma County per CCP 395. Mr. Wescott's request that the action remain in San Francisco based on the interests of justice per CCP 397 is premature as there is no answer on file. (Forster v. Superior Court (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 782, 788.). Order signed in open court. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk
